metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2794
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3: "Tetanus-induced nuclear accumulation of β-catenin"
- "we tested the hypothesis that activity-induced Wnt release activates intracellular signaling pathways"
- "Quantification of nuclear β-catenin signals of randomly selected DG granule cells showed that tetanus significantly increased β-catenin signals in the nuclei at 15 and 30 min after stimulations (Fig. 3, a-d)."
- "We also found that APV completely abolished tetanus-induced nuclear accumulations of β-catenin (Fig. 3, e-h), indicating an essential role of NMDA receptors in tetanus-induced activation of the canonical Wnt/β-catenin pathway. In support of the activation of Wnt/β-catenin signaling after tetanic stimulations, we observed, based on a DNA microarray analysis,3 that tetanus up-regulated mRNAs of several Wnt target genes (Fig. 3m); these Wnt target genes included Dll1 (41, 42), Wisp-2 (43), Cldn1 (44), Fas (45), and CDD44 (46)."
- "we investigated whether blockade of released Wnts can abolish tetanus-induced nuclear accumulation of β-catenin. To this end, we treated hippocampal slices with purified Frizzled-8-Fc (5 μg/ml), which is a Wnt scavenger (47). The results showed that Frizzled-8-Fc treatment abolished tetanus-induced nuclear accumulation of β-catenin (Fig. 3, i-l). \". Experimental description: - "Hippocampal slices (400 μm) were prepared from 3-4-week mice..."
- Species of recombinant Wnt3a used in Fig.5 is not stated in the paper.
